summary: Ukrainian drones are now striking targets up to 40 km from the front line, with loitering munitions targeting equipment within a 100 km radius, combining cheap mass-produced drones with expensive foreign models. Belarus has deployed armored vehicles with anti-drone protection, including the MZKT-233036-011 "Lis" and MZKT-490100-217, manufactured at the Minsk Wheel Tractor Plant. Russian anti-drone systems like "Elka" and "Yolka" have intercepted Ukrainian UAVs, with "Yolka" successfully engaging a target at 1,300 meters. The "Rubicon" Center reported destroying 116 Ukrainian targets, including fixed-wing UAVs, on April 6, 2026, in the Donbas and Krasny Liman directions.
